memory.summary: I'm way too stressed to ignore my friends, so I'll skip the easy chore and hang out with cohort_3 instead.

desires: Maintain the high trust and closeness with cohort_3 by continuing to prioritize our hangout over trivial tasks.
Avoid feeling like a 'reliable robot' by asserting my boundary that my social connection matters more than finishing this easy chore right now.
Seek immediate emotional relief from stress by staying in the moment with friends rather than planning for a deadline three days away.
Protect my current energy levels by refusing to switch gears to do work I can easily procrastinate on later.

goals.description: Immediately attend the 'leisure_now' event with cohort_3 to reinforce our bond and get emotional relief.
goals.rationale: My value for relationships (0.5) dwarfs academic concerns (0.15). I'm impulsive and need immediate stress relief now; ignoring a deadline three days away is worth it to maintain closeness and avoid feeling like a robot.

goals.description: Explicitly tell teammate_A or the group that I am skipping the quick_chore right now to focus on hanging out, asserting my boundary.
goals.rationale: I need to be assertive (8) about my priorities. Doing the chore just because it's easy feels like submitting to a task-oriented mindset I reject; I'll do it later when I feel like it, not because of a soft deadline.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances relationship bond (value 0.5) for immediate stress relief. I'm impulsive and prioritize closeness over the distant project deadline or a trivial chore. Skipping the chore asserts my boundary against task-only pressure.
